title: triticum intermedium subsp. trichophorum
intro: Triticum intermedium subsp. trichophorum, also known as wild emmer wheat or triticale, is a type of grass that belongs to the wheat family (Poaceae) and is native to the Mediterranean region. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including antifungal and antibacterial activities, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Triticum intermedium subsp. trichophorum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and seeds, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or added to food products.
